name: Split-Anchor Word-Slot · beat-synced kinetic typography
one_line: ONE base lockup — a HELD left anchor column of R rows (fixed words) beside a torn-paper word-SLOT box on the right — driven by five interchangeable beat-synced operators: anchor lock-in, slot word-group cycle (in/out, per-line color), full-scene background flip, global per-beat jitter, and a box-zoom exit wipe. R (rows) and the number of background flips are data, so 2/3/4 rows and 0/1/2 flips all 'just work'.

base_template.notes: A two-column kinetic-typography lockup. LEFT = an 'anchor' column of R held words (one per row, fixed for the whole group). RIGHT = a torn-paper 'slot' box (a colored card with a faint offset second sheet) holding up to R stacked text lines. R is derived from the `anchors` comma-list length; the box pre-builds maxRows line nodes from the program, so 2/3/4 rows follow the data with no code edit. A slot 'word-group' = the lines the box currently shows; cycling = swapping that group in/out. Asset-free: text + colored boxes only. All five operators below act on this one lockup — same DOM, different rhythmic scheduling.

lockup.intent: establish the held stage: pop the R left anchor words in (one per beat) and reveal the empty slot box
lockup.anchors: enter[] = onsets (one per anchor row, distributed); box = beat the empty box appears
lockup.what_moves: per-anchor pop-in (autoAlpha + y + scale); box scale-in. Anchors then hold for the whole group.

slot.intent: one word-GROUP cycle inside the box — lines slide in one-per-beat, hold, then slide out (omit exit to hold)
slot.anchors: enter[] = onsets (per line); exit[] = onsets (per line, or all equal for a one-shot clear)
slot.what_moves: per-line textContent set + slide/fade in, slide/fade out. `colors[]` tints individual lines (ACCENTS index) for the multi-color look; else the theme's boxInk.

bg.intent: flip the whole scene to another theme on a beat (stage bg + anchor ink + box fill + slot ink invert as one 0ms hit)
bg.what_moves: backgroundColor of root + box + sheet; color of anchors + slot lines. Schedule 0/1/2 of these = background changes none / once / twice.

shake.intent: global per-beat jitter of the whole lockup (x/y translate + rotation), one jolt per beat — rides a dense onset run / roll
shake.what_moves: transform of #lockup (anchors + box together); each jolt is a deterministic index-hashed offset that eases back to 0 by `settle`. Runs UNDER slot/bg (overlapping ops are fine — absolute-second scheduling).

expand.intent: the EXIT transition — fade the text out, reset the lockup transform, then ZOOM the slot box until it fills the frame (a color wipe into the next group)
expand.anchors: fade = beat text starts fading; at = box zoom start; to = box fills the frame
expand.what_moves: anchors + slot lines fade to 0; clip-path/rotation cleared; #slotbox scales to 30× (covers 1920×1080) ending opaque full-frame. Lands the black-out just before the next downbeat kick.

match.notes: Reach for this when the section is short, has a held idea (a brand / name to anchor on the left) and a stream of discrete onsets to pop separate words on, PLUS at least one dense run (roll / hihat fill) to ride a shake on and a strong downbeat to wipe out into. The five ops map onto: lock the name → cycle a couple of word-groups per beat → invert the palette on a key hit → jitter through the busy bit → zoom-wipe on the downbeat. Variable R (rows) and 0/1/2 bg flips are data, so it fits 2/3/4-line lockups and either a single or double color shift.

examples.program: lockup(0.125-0.5) → slot:'I bring/ideas/to life'(1.0-1.833) → slot:'you/wanna/see?'(2.042-2.75) → shake(2.16-4.18) → bg→green(3.458) → expand(4.3-4.792)

anchors.notes: comma list — ONE word per row; its length sets R (rows). 2/3/4 words = 2/3/4 rows.

theme.notes: THEMES index for the opening scene

program.notes: JSON array of operator steps (see program_format). Empty = built-in group_6 score. THIS is the compose-by-data surface: reorder/drop/repeat steps, swap beats, add bg flips — no code edit.

engine.model: operators + program-as-data (identical to poster-tile-mosaic). The 5 operators (§6 of index.html) are pure, parameterized functions that write GSAP tweens at ABSOLUTE audio seconds onto ONE paused timeline — so they are order-independent and may overlap (shake runs under slot/bg). A PROGRAM (data) lists which ops run on which beats; §7 dispatches OPS[step.op](step). Compose by editing DATA (the PROGRAM array, the `program` variable, or program.json) — never the operator code.
engine.dispatch: OPS[step.op](step) for each step in PROGRAM
engine.extend: add an operator = add a function + an OPS key; the program can then call it by name. Add themes to THEMES / accent colors to ACCENTS.

program_format._: A program is a JSON array of steps. Each step = { op, ...fields }. Beats are absolute audio seconds. theme = index into THEMES[]; colors[] = indices into ACCENTS[]. Rows (R) come from the `anchors` variable; the slot box auto-sizes to the widest word-group.

lockup.enter: number[] — one onset per anchor row (distributed if count != R); per-word pop-in
lockup.box: number?  — beat the empty slot box appears (default = last enter)
lockup.theme: number?  — THEMES index for the opening scene (default = base theme)

slot.words: string[] — the lines this cycle shows in the box (<= maxRows)
slot.enter: number[] — onset per line; slide/fade in
slot.exit: number[]? — onset per line to slide out; omit to HOLD the group. Equal values = a one-shot whole-group clear.
slot.colors: number[]? — ACCENTS index per line (the multi-color look); omit = theme boxInk
slot.theme: number?  — THEMES index whose boxInk colors the lines (default = base theme)

bg.at: number — beat the whole scene flips (0ms invert)
bg.theme: number — THEMES index to flip to. Add a 2nd `bg` step to change twice.

shake.beats: number[] — one jolt per beat (ride a roll / dense onset run)
shake.settle: number?  — seconds each jolt eases back to rest (default 0.12)

expand.fade: number  — beat the text starts fading out
expand.to: number? — beat the box fills the frame (default at + 0.15)
expand.theme: number? — recolor the box at zoom start (default = keep current)

program_data: program.json (the default group_6 score as standalone, agent-editable data)

provenance.audiomap: audiomap.json (scripts/analyze-beatgrid.py — ~92 BPM, downbeats 2.322/4.923, sustained hihat fill 2.16-4.18s)
provenance.video_read: group_6.gemini-analysis.md (Gemini 3 Pro 24fps) cross-checked by frame-by-frame contact sheets. Both agreed: NOT an image grid — pure kinetic typography. Left held 'studio/contre/courant'; right box cycled 'I bring/ideas/to life' then colored 'you/wanna/see?'; blue→green hard flip at f83; global shake on the hihat fill; box zoom-wipe to black at f112-114 into the f118 downbeat. (User's original read was correct except: it is the RIGHT slot box, not a 'left box', that zooms; and the text is masked/faded out, not a soft dissolve.)
